questions: When `usePinnedGroupSync` sees the shot's image list change, should it preserve the live timing of surviving clips and only append/remove members, or should it rebuild the full clip sequence for that group from shot order?

Should `(shotId, trackId)` remain the uniqueness key for a pinned group after the refactor, or can the same shot be pinned multiple times on the same track? This changes cross-track drag and repin behavior.

After deferring poll-sync or conflict reloads during an active drag/resize, should the editor immediately replay the newest deferred remote update when the gesture ends if local edits are clean, or just wait for the next poll/save cycle?

success_criteria.criterion: Loading a config with legacy `pinnedShotGroups[].children` yields runtime groups in the new soft-tag shape, and saving that same timeline emits no `start` or `children` fields.
No runtime load/save/commit path re-derives clip timing or track placement from shot-group child geometry; `clips[]` remains the only source of truth for `at` and duration.
Dragging the right edge of the last clip in a shot changes only that clip's duration and the group's rendered right boundary follows.
Dragging the left edge of the first clip in a shot changes only that clip's start/duration and the group's rendered left boundary follows.
Dragging an interior boundary inside a shot changes only the two adjacent clips while preserving the rest of the group.
Dragging a grouped clip body translates the full group together, and cross-track drag moves all grouped clips to the new track while updating the group's `trackId`.
Auto-save does not execute during an active drag or resize interaction, and the newest pending save flushes after the interaction ends.
Poll-sync and conflict reloads do not overwrite local edits during an active drag or resize interaction; deferred remote work is re-evaluated after the interaction ends.
Shot label menu actions and per-clip context-menu lockdown still work under the soft-tag model.
The video-editor test suite passes after updating intentional expectations, with no new failures beyond the known unrelated pre-existing TimelineCanvas assertion if it still remains.
Net LOC or helper surface across shot-group-specific files decreases after removing projection machinery.
An unchanged timeline produces the same clip-level data for Remotion preview before and after the refactor.
Manual browser smoke testing confirms the two reported resize/save-reset symptoms no longer reproduce.

assumptions: The brief's rollback direction is correct: a structural removal of projection is simpler and safer than more incremental fixes because projection is currently wired into load, save, commit, drag, resize, and rendering.
Until clarified, `(shotId, trackId)` remains the identity for a pinned group, and the refactor should not introduce a new stable group id.
Stored `clipIds` should remain ordered left-to-right by current live clip position whenever membership changes or a grouped move completes.
If save or poll work is deferred during an interaction, the implementation should keep only the latest pending item and re-evaluate it immediately once the interaction clears.
Unrelated long-lived diagnostics outside the shot-group and timeline-sync paths can stay; only temporary debugging logs added during this bug cycle should be removed as part of the focused rollback.
